title: Sayblohn

'''Sayblohn''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Quess sector/Legends|Quess sector]]. It was occupied by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and a [[Unidentified pirate group (Sayblohn)|group]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] had a [[Sayblohn pirate base|hidden base]] in the planet's [[Desert/Legends|badlands]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] team of [[operative]]s traveled to Sayblohn in order to retrieve a [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] [[Okfili art object|art object]]. After several run-ins with the local pirates, the team was ultimately successful in its mission.

==Description==
Sayblohn was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Sayblohn system/Legends|Sayblohn system]],<ref name="RPG2RE" /> a part of the [[Quess sector/Legends|Quess sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet's terrain included [[Desert/Legends|badlands]].<ref name="RPG2RE" />

==History==
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Sayblohn had been occupied by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="RPG2RE" />

{{RPGseed}}
At some point during that conflict, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] tasked<ref name="RPG2RE" /> a [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|special operative team]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|406|Okfili}}</ref> with retrieving from Sayblohn a [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] [[Okfili art object|art object]], which held significant religious importance to the [[Okfili]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Provided with the name and location of an Alliance [[Unidentified Alliance contact|contact]] on Sayblohn, the team traveled to the planet, only to be ambushed by a [[Unidentified pirate group (Sayblohn)|group]] of local [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]].<ref name="RPG2RE" />

Upon landing on Sayblohn, the [[operative]]s were questioned by Imperial authorities about the pirate attack the former had suffered. The Alliance team subsequently located its contact, finding him [[Death/Legends|murdered]]. When the agents discovered the [[Unidentified pirate leader (Sayblohn)|killer]], one of the pirates that had previously attacked them, they pursued him, locating the pirates' [[Sayblohn pirate base|hidden base]] in the process. A [[Lightfight|firefight]] ensued, and after their contact's murderer was defeated, the operatives [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] the stolen artifact onto their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] without attracting Imperial attention.<ref name="RPG2RE" />
{{Endgame}}

==Locations==
A group of pirates maintained a base on Sayblohn that was hidden in the planet's badlands.<ref name="RPG2RE" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tolonda-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|200px|Map from ''Voyages SF'' 13 depicting Sayblohn as part of the Tolonda sector.]]
Sayblohn was introduced in the [[1987]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|rulebook]] for the first edition of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Within the book, the planet is mentioned in an example of an outline for a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="TRPG1">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']]</ref>

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] roleplaying game source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Sayblohn system, and therefore Sayblohn itself, in the [[Tolonda sector/Legends|Tolonda sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' instead placed the Sayblohn system in grid square N-16,<ref name="TEA" /> which the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further depicted as being located outside the Tolonda [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC Outer Rim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>
